scope: Final review of full phase-1 Telegram obligations implementation on feature/telegram-phase1-obligations
summary: Fresh re-review after fix commit 8c7bee347315f96f6ed5e49146456b049d759186 found no remaining phase-1 blocking issues. Exact-target Mini App bootstrap is preserved for fresh opens, Mini home now matches the obligation engine's active-template scope, and the stated verification commands all pass on current HEAD.

commands.command: node --import tsx --test src/lib/journal-obligation-links.test.ts src/lib/journal-obligations.test.ts src/lib/bot/start-home.test.ts src/lib/bot/start-response.test.ts

npm run lint -- src/lib/journal-obligation-links.ts src/lib/journal-obligation-links.test.ts src/lib/journal-obligations.ts src/lib/journal-obligations.test.ts src/lib/bot/start-home.ts src/lib/bot/start-home.test.ts src/lib/bot/start-response.ts src/lib/bot/start-response.test.ts src/lib/bot/handlers/start.ts src/app/api/mini/home/route.ts src/app/mini/page.tsx src/app/mini/o/[id]/page.tsx

acceptanceCriteria.text: Telegram /start obligation CTA preserves exact-target behavior for fresh Mini App opens, including users without an existing session cookie.
Mini home reads the same active template set that the obligation sync/query layer uses, so home cards and obligation data stay aligned.
